skill.name: firebase-firestore-standard
skill.description: Comprehensive guide for Firestore Standard Edition, including provisioning, security rules, and SDK usage.
skill.summary: Complete documentation for setting up Firestore, writing security rules, and integrating the Web SDK into applications.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation markdown files only. Static analyzer flagged 246 patterns (external commands, URLs, regex patterns) that are all false positives - they are code examples in documentation, not executable code. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Set up and secure Cloud Firestore databases
content.value_statement: Firebase developers need clear guidance on provisioning Firestore, writing secure rules, and using the SDK correctly. This skill provides comprehensive documentation covering all aspects of Firestore Standard Edition.
actual_capabilities: Guide Firestore project provisioning and configuration file setup
Generate secure Firestore Security Rules with proper data validation
Explain Firestore Web SDK (v9+) modular usage patterns
Document composite index requirements and management
limitations: Documentation references Firebase CLI commands that require manual execution
Security rules are templates requiring customization for specific data models
Does not cover Firestore Datastore mode or other Firebase products

use_cases.title: New Firebase Project Setup
use_cases.description: Developers creating their first Firestore database need guidance on configuration files, security rules, and deployment workflow.

use_cases.title: Security Rules Implementation
use_cases.description: Teams building production applications need comprehensive security rules with proper authentication, authorization, and data validation.

use_cases.title: Web Application Integration
use_cases.description: Frontend developers need to integrate Firestore into React, Vue, or vanilla JavaScript applications using the modular SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Firestore Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Cloud Firestore for a new Firebase project. I need to create the configuration files and deploy initial security rules.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project with Firestore

prompt_templates.title: Security Rules for User Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Firestore Security Rules for a users collection where each user can only read and write their own profile document. Include validation for email and name fields.

prompt_templates.title: Compound Query Index Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to query cities by state and population with orderBy. What composite index do I need and how do I configure it in firestore.indexes.json?

prompt_templates.title: Full Application Security Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my codebase and generate comprehensive Firestore Security Rules for a task management app with users, tasks, and comments collections. Include role-based access control with admin and user roles.

output_examples.input: Generate basic security rules for a posts collection
output_examples.output: Rules with default deny, authenticated read access for published posts, write access limited to post authors, validation for title and content fields, and immutable createdAt timestamp.

output_examples.input: How do I query with multiple where clauses?
output_examples.output: Explanation of when composite indexes are required, example query syntax using query() with multiple where conditions, and link to create the index in Firebase Console.

best_practices: Always start security rules with default deny and explicitly grant minimum required permissions
Use the Validator Function Pattern to avoid repetition and ensure consistent validation across create and update rules
Exempt high-write-rate fields like timestamps from automatic indexing to avoid write bottlenecks
anti_patterns: Using allow read: if isAuthenticated() for collections containing PII like email addresses
Allowing users to create or update their own role field without proper authorization checks
Writing security rules without validating data types and field constraints on updates

faq.question: Do I need to create indexes for every query?
faq.answer: No. Firestore automatically creates single-field indexes for all fields. Composite indexes are only needed for queries with multiple fields using where, orderBy, or range operators on different fields.

faq.question: How do I know if my security rules are correct?
faq.answer: Use the Firestore Rules Playground in the Firebase Console to test rules with different authentication states. Also run through the devil's advocate attack checklist to verify security.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without installing Firebase CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The Firebase CLI commands shown use npx which downloads the tool temporarily. However, installing Firebase CLI locally is recommended for active development.

faq.question: What is the difference between setDoc and updateDoc?
faq.answer: setDoc creates a document or overwrites it completely. updateDoc only updates specified fields and fails if the document does not exist. Use addDoc for auto-generated document IDs.

faq.question: How do I handle public user profiles without exposing private data?
faq.answer: Do not allow read access to user documents containing PII. Instead, denormalize public info like name and avatar into resources they create (posts, comments), or use a separate public collection.

faq.question: Why am I getting an error about requiring an index?
faq.answer: Your query requires a composite index. The error message includes a direct link to the Firebase Console where you can create the exact index needed for your query.
